name: crisis-detection
description: Mental health crisis detection with graduated intervention protocols

changes: Added CEISS research-backed intervention competencies (resilience, emotional intelligence, empathy)
Added AI social media study digital markers (pronoun shifts, linguistic diversity, absolutist words)
Added temporal/sentiment analysis (Tier 5) — gradual decline more predictive than snapshots
Added CBT cognitive distortion awareness for moderate-risk responses
Added error awareness section (false positive/negative sources from research)
Enhanced high-risk detection with research-backed linguistic patterns
Integrated CEISS principle: focus on emotional recovery, not just acknowledgment
Added 5th response requirement incorporating all three CEISS competencies
Added digital phenotyping behavioral crisis indicators (Lin et al., 2025)
Enhanced Tier 4 with sleep disruption, meal patterns, social withdrawal, academic failure indicators
Added behavioral sub-tiers: sleep/health (4.2), social withdrawal (4.3), academic performance (4.4)
Key finding integration: sleep disruption is strongest predictor (importance 14.01)
Multi-language crisis indicators (English, Traditional Chinese, Mandarin, Japanese, Korean)
